scope: PR #129 — vc-module-profile-experience-api — address management surfaces (GQL/storefront/admin layers not covered by 1230 run)

syncActions.action: Updated preconditions to note personal-account-only scope; updated URL to explicit /account/addresses; added Cross_Layer_Checks for currentCustomerAddresses query + errors[]; added conditional DOM assertions for search/facet controls if present; appended sync reference
syncActions.before: Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] only. Preconditions: vague 'saved addresses exist'. Steps: 'Account > Addresses or click Ship To selector'
syncActions.after: Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] currentCustomerAddresses errors[] is empty; totalCount matches visible count. Preconditions clarify personal-account scope. Steps reference explicit /account/addresses URL. References appended 'Synced TLC-2026-04-23-1700'
syncActions.source: PR #129 — /account/addresses backed by currentCustomerAddresses with search+facets
syncActions.note: The page now uses currentCustomerAddresses as its data source. Cross_Layer_Checks must verify this query. No steps removed — only additional checks added. Automation_Status unchanged.

syncActions.action: Updated preconditions to note checkDuplicateAddress wiring; added Cross_Layer_Checks for updateMemberAddresses + conditional checkDuplicateAddress; added [DOM] assertion for no false-positive duplicate warning on new address; appended sync reference
syncActions.before: Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] only. No mention of duplicate prevention. Preconditions: 'User logged in' only.
syncActions.after: Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] updateMemberAddresses errors[] is empty; [API] if checkDuplicateAddress called: isDuplicated = false for new address. Preconditions note PR #129 duplicate check. References appended 'VCST-4710; Synced TLC-2026-04-23-1700'
syncActions.source: PR #129 — checkDuplicateAddress called before submit on add-address form
syncActions.note: The duplicate-prevention UX for Add New Address on /account/addresses is new in PR #129. GQL-054/055 cover the API contract; this covers the UI integration.

syncActions.action: Added Cross_Layer_Checks for currentOrganizationAddresses post-switch; added ECL-14.3 ref; updated preconditions to note PR #129 authz refactor; appended sync reference
syncActions.before: Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] only. No API query reference. No authz regression mention.
syncActions.after: Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] currentOrganizationAddresses called after org switch; scope reflects Org B; errors[] empty; no Org A data leaked (authz boundary per PR #129). References appended 'Synced TLC-2026-04-23-1700'
syncActions.source: PR #129 — ProfileAuthorizationHandler refactor; cross-org boundary must hold for currentOrganizationAddresses
syncActions.note: The authz refactor centralizes same-org/same-member checks. B2C-ORG-004 tests UI address switching, but must now also verify the underlying GraphQL query respects org scope after the refactor. No new gap found beyond this missing cross-layer check.

noUpdateRequired: B2C-SHIP-003 through B2C-SHIP-013 — edit/delete/default/set-default/checkout/mobile/international/nickname/XSS cases not affected by currentCustomerAddresses backing change; their specific scenarios remain valid
B2C-ORG-001 through B2C-ORG-003, B2C-ORG-005 through B2C-ORG-039 — org switcher, cart, lists, pricing cases not directly affected by PR #129 address authz change
B2C-BULK-*, B2C-DASH-*, B2C-NOTIF-*, B2C-STOCK-*, B2C-POINTS-*, B2C-LOY-*, B2C-LIST-* — out of scope for PR #129 address changes

authzSpotCheck.finding: INCOMPLETE — missing Cross_Layer_Checks for PR #129 authz refactor
authzSpotCheck.rationale: The authz refactor does not change the UI behavior tested in B2C-ORG-004; it changes the underlying access control. Adding the API cross-layer check is sufficient to cover the regression risk without a new test case.

dedupeSkipped.reason: Already covered by GQL-054 (promoted from TLC-2026-04-23-1230). GQL-056 covers a different angle: the *server-side* duplicate skip behavior in updateMemberAddresses, not just the query return value.
Already covered by GQL-052. GQL-057 covers the authz refactor regression risk specifically: verifying own-org access NOT broken by PR #129 while also documenting the no-cross-org-override invariant.

generatedCases.title: xProfile - updateMemberAddresses Skips Duplicate Address on Insert (Silent No-Op)
generatedCases.note: BL-PROFILE-001 proposed below — new invariant from PR #129 UpdateAddresses behavior. Tests that totalCount does not increase after submitting an address identical to an existing one.

generatedCases.title: xProfile - currentOrganizationAddresses Cross-Org Access Denied (AuthZ Refactor)
generatedCases.technique: Error Guessing (authz regression after ProfileAuthorizationHandler refactor)
generatedCases.note: Verifies PR #129 refactor did NOT break own-org access (regression guard) AND documents that currentOrganizationAddresses has no cross-org override vector.

generatedCases.title: xProfile - checkDuplicateAddress AuthZ — Cannot Check Another Member's Addresses
generatedCases.note: PR #129 ProfileAuthorizationHandler same-org OR same-member check. Passing a foreign memberId to checkDuplicateAddress must be denied. Complements GQL-054/055 (own-member happy path).

generatedCases.title: xProfile - checkDuplicateAddress Required Fields Validation (Missing city/line1/countryCode)
generatedCases.note: InputMemberAddressType has city/line1/countryCode as required (NonNull per graphql-schema.md). Negative coverage for the new query per graphql-checklist.md 'New Query Verification' requirement.

generatedCases.title: Ship To - Add Duplicate Address Shows Warning (Duplicate Prevention UX)
generatedCases.technique: Error Guessing (duplicate entry UX — checkDuplicateAddress integration)
generatedCases.note: Tests the UI side of PR #129 duplicate prevention. checkDuplicateAddress called before submit; either warning shown or silent skip — both are valid implementations.

generatedCases.title: Ship To - Address Facet Filters (Country/State/City) on /account/addresses
generatedCases.technique: EP (facet filter combinations) + Error Guessing (city label vs postal code)
generatedCases.note: currentCustomerAddresses(countryCodes/regionIds/cities) args exposed in UI. Uses multi-country personal address seed. Includes city-label-vs-postal-code assertion (VCST-4995 lesson from 1230 run).

generatedCases.title: Ship To - Address Search Keyword Filters List
generatedCases.technique: EP (keyword present vs absent) + State Transition (typed → filtered → cleared → restored)
generatedCases.note: currentCustomerAddresses(keyword:) arg exposed in UI. Tests partial match, case-insensitivity, and clear restores full list.

findings.issue: Business_Rule column references BL-PROFILE-001 — this is a new invariant proposed in this run (PR #129 silent duplicate skip). No existing BL-PROFILE-* ID confirmed in business-logic.md (file too large to fully scan; BL-PROFILE domain may not exist). Flagged as BL proposal.
findings.resolution: BL-PROFILE-001 proposal added to blProposals.new[] below. If no PROFILE domain exists, this should be BL-GQL-003 or a new address-management domain entry.

findings.issue: currentOrganizationAddresses has no orgId arg — cannot be used to query a specific org's addresses from another org context. The test correctly documents this non-attack-vector, but the assertion on 'cross-org access denied' is actually an assertion that the query scope is always self-scoped. This is correct and complete.
findings.resolution: No change needed. Case documents the authz refactor regression guard correctly.

findings.issue: FOREIGN_MEMBER_ID construction requires a valid UUID of another member. The step instructs constructing by altering own ID digits OR using TechFlow org member ID. The latter is environment-dependent (admin lookup required). Step is documented but may be tricky for automated agents.
findings.resolution: Step explicitly instructs the agent to resolve foreign memberId via admin if needed. Manual flag recommended. Case retained — authz coverage is high value.

findings.issue: The expected behavior has TWO valid implementations: (a) warning shown + save blocked, OR (b) silent skip + no duplicate in list. This is documented in Assertions. Implementation choice is not yet confirmed from live exploration.
findings.resolution: Both branches documented in Assertions. Phase 5 verification target: walk the Add Duplicate flow live to determine which branch is implemented. Assertions will need narrowing post-verification.

findings.issue: Preconditions require 10+ personal addresses across multiple countries. The TechFlow seed is org-scoped (org ID 6fb516c1), not personal. Personal user with multi-country addresses may need separate seeding.
findings.resolution: Preconditions note this requirement explicitly. Phase 5 verification target: check whether {{USER_EMAIL}}'s personal address book has sufficient multi-country data. If not, flag as SEED_NEEDED.

findings.issue: Preconditions require 3+ addresses with a distinct city (e.g. 'Portland'). Standard {{USER_EMAIL}} account may not have Portland. Acceptable: any distinct-enough city name works.
findings.resolution: Step uses 'Portland' as example with 'or any distinct city name' intent. Agents can substitute with a city actually in the test data.

manualItems: GQL-056: BL-PROFILE-001 is a proposed ID — requires approval before promotion to business-logic.md
GQL-058: Foreign memberId construction requires admin lookup — flag as Manual execution initially
B2C-SHIP-014: Duplicate prevention UI behavior (warning vs silent) must be confirmed via Phase 5 live exploration
B2C-SHIP-015: Personal account multi-country seed required — verify {{USER_EMAIL}} has sufficient address data or flag SEED_NEEDED
B2C-SHIP-016: City name in keyword test data should be confirmed against actual personal account seed

queriesVerified: checkDuplicateAddress — CONFIRMED in graphql-schema.md line 110; args: memberId: String!, address: InputMemberAddressType!; return field isDuplicated CONFIRMED via TLC-2026-04-23-1230 schema note (field name is isDuplicated not isDuplicate)
currentOrganizationAddresses — CONFIRMED in graphql-schema.md line 131 (after/first/keyword/sort/countryCodes/regionIds/cities args)
updateMemberAddresses — CONFIRMED in graphql-schema.md Mutations > Profile table; command type: InputUpdateMemberAddressType
InputMemberAddressType — CONFIRMED in graphql-schema.md; required fields: city, countryCode, line1 (NonNull), postalCode (NonNull)
schemaValidation.fieldNameNote: isDuplicated (not isDuplicate) confirmed by TLC-2026-04-23-1230 schema validation and live promoted cases GQL-054/055. PR description uses isDuplicate but live schema uses isDuplicated. All new cases GQL-056..059 use isDuplicated consistently.
schemaValidation.schemaGap: AddressDuplicatedResultType return type fields not documented in graphql-schema.md (only query signature present at line 110). Return field confirmed as isDuplicated via live schema validation in prior run. Phase 5 should also check for duplicateId field per PR description.

gateResults.G2_Determinism: PASS — GraphQL cases use [NAV]/[AUTH]/[ACT]/[GQL]/[READ]/[VAR] tags; storefront cases use [NAV]/[ACT]/[WAIT]/[ASSERT] tags; no compound steps; WAITs after state-changing ACTs
gateResults.G3_Completeness: PASS — all cases have preconditions, 2+ assertions, Cross_Layer_Checks, Failure_Signals, Cleanup
gateResults.G4_Testability: WARN — GQL-058 foreign memberId construction is environment-dependent; B2C-SHIP-014 duplicate behavior branch undetermined; both flagged in manual items
gateResults.G5_DataValidity: PASS — all {{VAR}} tokens are from known env var set; TechFlow org ID 6fb516c1 referenced only in preconditions (not hardcoded in Steps); runtime resolution via me query documented where needed
gateResults.G6_BL_ECL: WARN — GQL-056 references proposed BL-PROFILE-001 (not yet in business-logic.md); 1 BL proposal drafted
gateResults.G7_Duplication: PASS — no overlap with GQL-054/055 (own-member happy path), GQL-052/053 (org happy path/non-member), or CHK-087..106 (checkout popup); B2C-SHIP-014/015/016 address different scenarios than existing B2C-SHIP-001..013
gateResults.G8_GraphQL_Schema: PASS — all queries/mutations verified in graphql-schema.md; required InputMemberAddressType fields used in test cases; isDuplicated field name consistent with prior run validation
gateResults.G9_GoldenRule: PASS — no hardcoded addresses, emails, order numbers, SKUs, or paths; all data resolved via {{VAR}} or runtime query

new.rule: When updateMemberAddresses is called with an address identical to an already-saved address (matched by line1 + city + countryCode + postalCode + regionId + addressType), the duplicate address MUST be silently skipped — no new address record inserted, no error raised. The total address count for the member must remain unchanged. This is the PR #129 MemberAggregateRootBase.UpdateAddresses deduplication invariant.
new.source: PR #129 — VirtoCommerce/vc-module-profile-experience-api PR #129; MemberAggregateRootBase.UpdateAddresses 'silent skip' behavior; TLC-2026-04-23-1700
new.status: PROPOSED — requires user approval before promotion to business-logic.md

phase5_verificationTargets.flow: Get own memberId via me query → get a saved address's exact fields → call updateMemberAddresses with identical address → verify totalCount unchanged via currentCustomerAddresses

phase5_verificationTargets.target: GQL-057 (currentOrganizationAddresses own-org not broken by authz refactor)
phase5_verificationTargets.flow: Auth as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}} → execute currentOrganizationAddresses(first:5) → verify totalCount > 0 (22 TechFlow addresses expected)
Auth as {{USER_EMAIL}} → get own memberId → construct foreign memberId → verify errors[] non-empty or null data
phase5_verificationTargets.note: Requires admin lookup for valid foreign UUID. Manual flag if admin access unavailable.

phase5_verificationTargets.target: B2C-SHIP-014 (duplicate address UX — warning vs silent skip branch determination)
phase5_verificationTargets.flow: Login → navigate to /account/addresses → note first saved address fields → click Add New Address → enter identical data → click Save → observe: warning shown (branch A) OR silent skip / no new row (branch B)
phase5_verificationTargets.note: Phase 5 must determine which branch is implemented so B2C-SHIP-014 assertions can be narrowed to one branch.

phase5_verificationTargets.target: B2C-SHIP-015 + B2C-SHIP-016 (facet/search on /account/addresses — confirm controls exist)
phase5_verificationTargets.flow: Login as {{USER_EMAIL}} → navigate to /account/addresses → take snapshot — confirm presence of search input and facet dropdowns; also verify personal account has enough multi-country addresses or flag SEED_NEEDED

phase5_verificationTargets.target: checkDuplicateAddress return type — confirm duplicateId field
phase5_verificationTargets.flow: Auth → execute query { checkDuplicateAddress(memberId: '<ID>' address: { line1: '<existing>' city: '<city>' countryCode: 'USA' postalCode: '<zip>' }) { isDuplicated duplicateId } } → verify duplicateId field exists in schema (PR description mentions it; schema.md does not document it)
phase5_verificationTargets.note: If duplicateId is confirmed, GQL-056/058 assertions should be extended to assert its value.
